channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iranian ports. And, Orbán loses Hungarian election
item.description: President Trump announced a blockade of Iranian ports after peace talks with Iran collapsed. And, Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power in Hungary.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump announced a blockade of Iranian ports after peace talks with Iran collapsed. And, Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power in Hungary.

(Image credit: Attila Kisbenedek)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The U.S. military is about to block ships from Iran's ports after talks failed
item.description: The U.S. Central Command said the blockade would be "enforced impartially against vessels of all nations" from 10 a.m. EDT, following the breakdown of talks after 21 hours of negotiations.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. Central Command said the blockade would be "enforced impartially against vessels of all nations" from 10 a.m. EDT, following the breakdown of talks after 21 hours of negotiations.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iranian ports after peace talks fail
item.description: On Sunday, President Trump said the U.S. would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations between the U.S. and Iran broke down over the weekend.
item.content:encoded: On Sunday, President Trump said the U.S. would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations between the U.S. and Iran broke down over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Retired U.S. Navy admiral on Trump's threat to blockade the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to retired U.S. Navy Adm. James Foggo, dean of the Center for Maritime Strategy, about President Trump's command to blockade the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to retired U.S. Navy Adm. James Foggo, dean of the Center for Maritime Strategy, about President Trump's command to blockade the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: What a chimpanzee 'civil war' can teach us about how societies fall apart
item.description: A long-term study of the world's largest known community of chimpanzees has documented a rare event: what the researchers describe as the primate equivalent of a "civil war."
item.content:encoded:
A long-term study of the world's largest known community of chimpanzees has documented a rare event: what the researchers describe as the primate equivalent of a "civil war."

(Image credit: Aaron Sandel)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: How a $75 billion windfall from Congress has insulated ICE
item.description: Last year, Congress approved $75 billion for immigration enforcement. That money has allowed ICE to operate nearly unfettered during a record-long shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security.
item.content:encoded:
Last year, Congress approved $75 billion for immigration enforcement. That money has allowed ICE to operate nearly unfettered during a record-long shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security.

(Image credit: Stephen Maturen)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: More than a quarter of private colleges are at risk of closing, a new projection shows
item.description: As one Vermont college finishes its last semester, an estimated 442 others may be in trouble.
item.content:encoded:
As one Vermont college finishes its last semester, an estimated 442 others may be in trouble.

(Image credit: Oliver Parini for The Hechinger Report)
item.dc:creator: Jon Marcus

item.title: The FAA takes a rare step to head off a traffic jam at Chicago's O'Hare Airport
item.description: Federal regulators want airlines to cut the number of flights at O'Hare Airport in Chicago this summer. It's an unusual move, sparked by a turf war between two major airlines with hubs at the airport.
item.content:encoded:
Federal regulators want airlines to cut the number of flights at O'Hare Airport in Chicago this summer. It's an unusual move, sparked by a turf war between two major airlines with hubs at the airport.

(Image credit: Daniel Slim)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: 2025 was one of most volatile years ever for U.S. naturalizations
item.description: The year began with many people becoming U.S. citizens, but by December, fewer people were doing so, driven by ramped-up scrutiny of applications and eroding trust in the system.
item.content:encoded:
The year began with many people becoming U.S. citizens, but by December, fewer people were doing so, driven by ramped-up scrutiny of applications and eroding trust in the system.

(Image credit: Joseph Prezioso)
item.dc:creator: Anusha Mathur

item.title: Can red light therapy really deliver a beauty and health glow-up? Here's the science
item.description: Wellness influencers tout the therapy's power for everything from rejuvenating skin and hair to boosting longevity. Devices sold for at-home use abound. We look at the evidence behind the hype.
item.content:encoded: Wellness influencers tout the therapy's power for everything from rejuvenating skin and hair to boosting longevity. Devices sold for at-home use abound. We look at the evidence behind the hype.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy
